---
title: 'Resolution... DTR... Shelley'
date: '2019-05-30T07:57:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '841877260717694652'
blogger_filename: '/2019/05/resolution-dtr-shelley.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
---

I THINK I resolved things with Shelley tonight... I think she finally heard me, to the best of her current ability. She lacks the skills to communicate at an emotional level. I did too until Bob Hamp taught me how. But I think she heard me. It was a thorough Determine The Relationship (DTR) and we are both clear on where I stand now. I'm not entirely sure she knows where she stands. I think that's the point of this season. She's exploring herself. She was probably hoping to hold on to me and still explore with the other hand... but I was already too far gone for that. I have to change things, lead, and that meant setting things straight between us. I now feel confident I can be her friend and only her friend. It is what it is. Maybe God will bring us back together in that way someday. I'm not really sure anymore. I just know I had to do a HARD reset before I could go on and move forward. Time to just be single, I guess.<br />
<br />

<span class="_40fu" data-hover="none" style="direction: ltr; float: right; font-family: inherit;"><span class="_1z_2 _2u_d" style="align-items: center; display: flex; flex-direction: row-reverse; font-family: inherit; position: absolute; right: 359.648px; top: 809.409px; transform: translate(-10%, -50%); width: 100px;"></span></span><div aria-label="We were friends and that worked.

In February we crossed a line into something more. You sent pictures, I felt kind of pursued me a little (from my perspective) and practically blew up my phone non stop. Everytime I tried to back off a little, you said &quot;Go ahead, you ain't gonna find nothing better than this&quot;... And you worked your way into the place I'd been keeping protected. 

I actually became convinced there was more happening. I started drafting a courtship plan, I even crafted a plan to propose. 

You kept telling me how you wanted to attack my face.

From my perspective, you took a sharp 180* after the divorce. You needed space and needed to not be peed on. I got that. You even said I was 8 months ahead of you. You wanted to be &quot;just friends&quot;... I agreed for your sake but my heart never lined up with that decision.

Then you were talking apps and making out just for fun... And I understood, I did that too when I got single. In my own way.

Then you just straight dissappeared for a while and I lost my friend. I went to the dark place and all that comes with it.

We found a new place and I started to feel like I had you back.

Then Scott came into the picture.

Then he was over all the time.

Then you dissappeared again. All the texts and daily semi daily talk stopped.

Then you needed me, I thought, but turned around and went on a date when you'd just said he was causing you problems.

Now you gave away our night. You've generally only missed that because you fell asleep.

I've never once not sat here waiting on Sunday evenings. Not once. Except for the one time I told you ahead of time I had a conflicting schedule item.

.......


I'm feeling cast aside. Second best. Like the back-up friend... 

I fell for you... And then you backed off... Slowly, then abruptly.

.....

I am going to be okay, but frankly, I feel more like an ex boyfriend than a best friend.

I'll get over it.

I'll heal.

I'll be here for you.

I just don't know what any of that means right now, practically.

The hurt side says I'd rather you didn't come to visit because I don't think I could handle it.

So I'm going to be okay, but I'm not okay right now. I actually feel a little led on.

I will be here when you need me, but, I need to figure out what this new thing is supposed to look like. 

I can't be having &quot;date night&quot; on my schedule for &quot;just friends&quot;.

Please give careful prayer to what lines or boundaries you need too, and let me know.

I just need time to reset. I guess." class="_aok" style="font-family: inherit; outline: none;" tabindex="0">
<span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">We were friends and that worked.

In February we crossed a line into something more. You sent pictures, I felt kind of pursued me a little (from my perspective) and practically blew up my phone non stop. Everytime I tried to back off a little, you said "Go ahead, you ain't gonna find nothing better than this"... And you worked your way into the place I'd been keeping protected. 

I actually became convinced there was more happening. I started drafting a courtship plan, I even crafted a plan to propose. 

You kept telling me how you wanted to attack my face.

From my perspective, you took a sharp 180* after the divorce. You needed space and needed to not be peed on. I got that. You even said I was 8 months ahead of you. You wanted to be "just friends"... I agreed for your sake but my heart never lined up with that decision.

Then you were talking apps and making out just for fun... And I understood, I did that too when I got single. In my own way.

Then you just straight dissappeared for a while and I lost my friend. I went to the dark place and all that comes with it.

We found a new place and I started to feel like I had you back.

Then Scott came into the picture.

Then he was over all the time.

Then you dissappeared again. All the texts and daily semi daily talk stopped.

Then you needed me, I thought, but turned around and went on a date when you'd just said he was causing you problems.

Now you gave away our night. You've generally only missed that because you fell asleep.

I've never once not sat here waiting on Sunday evenings. Not once. Except for the one time I told you ahead of time I had a conflicting schedule item.

.......


I'm feeling cast aside. Second best. Like the back-up friend... 

I fell for you... And then you backed off... Slowly, then abruptly.

.....

I am going to be okay, but frankly, I feel more like an ex boyfriend than a best friend.

I'll get over it.

I'll heal.

I'll be here for you.

I just don't know what any of that means right now, practically.

The hurt side says I'd rather you didn't come to visit because I don't think I could handle it.

So I'm going to be okay, but I'm not okay right now. I actually feel a little led on.

I will be here when you need me, but, I need to figure out what this new thing is supposed to look like. 

I can't be having "date night" on my schedule for "just friends".

Please give careful prayer to what lines or boundaries you need too, and let me know.

I just need time to reset. I guess.</span></div>

<div style="font-family: inherit;">

<div class="_1t_q" style="font-family: inherit;">
<div class="_7934 _150g uiPopover _6a _6b" style="cursor: pointer; display: inline-block; font-family: inherit; vertical-align: unset;">
<div aria-controls="js_vi" class="_4ldz _1t_r _p" data-hover="tooltip" data-tooltip-content="Shelley Hunter" data-tooltip-position="left" id="js_u6" role="button" style="bottom: 2px; font-family: inherit; height: 32px; max-height: 28px; max-width: 28px; position: absolute; width: 32px;">

<div class="_41ud" style="font-family: inherit; margin-left: 36px;">
<div class="clearfix _o46 _3erg _29_7 direction_ltr text_align_ltr" style="direction: ltr; font-family: inherit; position: relative; text-align: left; white-space: pre-wrap; zoom: 1;">
<div class="_o46 _3erg _29_7 _3egs" style="background: white; font-family: inherit; position: relative; white-space: pre-wrap;">
<div class="_3058 _4k7a _3-9b direction_ltr" style="clear: left; color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5); direction: ltr; float: left; font-family: inherit; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 2px; margin-left: 12px; overflow-wrap: break-word;">
<img alt="" class="_6e38 _3-8_ img" height="8" src="/images/imported/static-xx-fbcdn-net/mxoh6bjsctl-0b84f5ccb245.png" style="border: 0px; margin-right: 4px;" width="8" />Shelley replied to you</div>
</div>
<div class="_3058 _ui9 _hh7 _6ybn _s1- _52mr _3oh- _6uum" data-hover="tooltip" data-tooltip-content="Monday 11:28am" data-tooltip-position="left" style="background-color: #f1f0f0; border-radius: 1.3em; box-sizing: content-box; clear: left; color: black; float: left; font-family: inherit; margin: 1px 0px; max-width: 55%; outline: none; overflow-wrap: break-word; padding: 6px 12px 7px; position: relative; user-select: text;">
<span class="_40fu" data-hover="none" style="direction: ltr; float: left; font-family: inherit;"><span class="_1z_2 _2u_d" style="display: flex; font-family: inherit; left: 359.648px; position: absolute; top: 54.6818px; transform: translate(10%, -50%); width: 110px;"></span></span><a class="_6e6n" href="https://www.facebook.com/messages/t/100006410115712#" style="color: black; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; text-decoration: none;"><blockquote class="_pye" style="color: #828282; display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; margin: 6px 0px; max-width: 100%; overflow-wrap: break-word; padding-left: 10px; position: relative; white-space: pre-wrap;">
<div class="_pyf" style="background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border-radius: 1px; display: inline-block; font-family: inherit; height: 32px; margin-left: -10px; position: absolute; width: 2px;">
</div>
<div class="_4k7e _4ik4 _4ik5" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; float: none; font-family: inherit; line-height: 16px; max-height: 32px; overflow: hidden; white-space: normal;">
<span style="font-family: inherit;"><span style="font-family: inherit;">We were friends and that worked.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">In February we crossed a line into something more. You sent pictures, I felt kind of pursued me a little (from my perspective) and practically blew up my phone non stop. Everytime I tried to back off a little, you said "Go ahead, you ain't gonna find nothing better than this"... And you worked your way into the place I'd been keeping protected.<span>&nbsp;</span></span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I actually became convinced there was more happening. I started drafting a courtship plan, I even crafted a plan to propose.<span>&nbsp;</span></span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">You kept telling me how you wanted to attack my face.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">From my perspective, you took a sharp 180* after the divorce. You needed space and needed to not be peed on. I got that. You even said I was 8 months ahead of you. You wanted to be "just friends"... I agreed for your sake but my heart never lined up with that decision.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Then you were talking apps and making out just for fun... And I understood, I did that too when I got single. In my own way.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Then you just straight dissappeared for a while and I lost my friend. I went to the dark place and all that comes with it.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">We found a new place and I started to feel like I had you back.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Then Scott came into the picture.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Then he was over all the time.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Then you dissappeared again. All the texts and daily semi daily talk stopped.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Then you needed me, I thought, but turned around and went on a date when you'd just said he was causing you problems.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Now you gave away our night. You've generally only missed that because you fell asleep.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I've never once not sat here waiting on Sunday evenings. Not once. Except for the one time I told you ahead of time I had a conflicting schedule item.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">.......</span><br /><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I'm feeling cast aside. Second best. Like the back-up friend...<span>&nbsp;</span></span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I fell for you... And then you backed off... Slowly, then abruptly.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">.....</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I am going to be okay, but frankly, I feel more like an ex boyfriend than a best friend.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I'll get over it.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I'll heal.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I'll be here for you.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I just don't know what any of that means right now, practically.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">The hurt side says I'd rather you didn't come to visit because I don't think I could handle it.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">So I'm going to be okay, but I'm not okay right now. I actually feel a little led on.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I will be here when you need me, but, I need to figure out what this new thing is supposed to look like.<span>&nbsp;</span></span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I can't be having "date night" on my schedule for "just friends".</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Please give careful prayer to what lines or boundaries you need too, and let me know.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">I just need time to reset. I guess.</span></span></div>
</blockquote>
</a><div aria-label="Good thoughts.

I am in an interesting season  ......" class="_aok" style="font-family: inherit; outline: none;" tabindex="0">
<span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">Good thoughts.

I am in an interesting season  ......</span></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div style="font-family: inherit;">
<div class="_1t_p clearfix" style="font-family: inherit; margin-bottom: 15px; position: relative; zoom: 1;">
<div class="_41ud" style="font-family: inherit; margin-left: 36px;">
<h5 aria-label="Darrell" class="_ih3 accessible_elem _-ne" style="clip: rect(1px, 1px, 1px, 1px); color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4); font-family: inherit; font-size: 12px; font-weight: normal; height: 1px; line-height: 1.28; margin: 0px 0px 1px; opacity: 0; overflow: hidden; padding: 0px 0px 0px 12px; position: absolute; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; width: 1px;">

<span style="font-family: inherit;"><span style="font-family: inherit;">That means I need to see you differently. I need to stop expecting you to make me the first person you tell anything too. <span style="font-family: inherit;"><span style="font-family: inherit;">But I have lost the dream I was holding on to a little too tightly. That died this week. And it needed to if I'm going to be able to allow you the space to be single.</span></span></div>

</a><div aria-label="Because watching you date AND being In Love with you are not two states I can hold at the same time. It literally kills me a little. So if I am going to be your friend and honor you and give you the space you need in this season... I cannot do so with a dream of marrying you when you are through some day. If I am going to actually give you that space I need to close that door in my heart. Our friendship isn't either or... That dream is." class="_aok" style="font-family: inherit; outline: none;" tabindex="0">

<span style="font-family: inherit;"><span style="font-family: inherit;">I was planning on getting away once they were settled in.</span><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">It is not like I was in his lap.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">The children were wanting it and I needed the time for them to transition.......the call came in over 2 hours earlier than normal and I answered so I could make a special effort to pay attention to what you needed.</span></span></div>
</blockquote>
</a><div aria-label="#1... I called exactly three minutes after our normal scheduled start time. Typically, you have the babies in bed by 7 my time, 9 your time. I usually check in about 7 and you are running a bath and then we pick up the phone between 7-7:30 ish... It's been your schedule item for when you set it. We've started later but usually we start about that time." class="_aok" style="font-family: inherit; outline: none;" tabindex="0">
<span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">#1... I called exactly three minutes after our normal scheduled start time. It's been your schedule item for when you set it. We've started later but usually we start about that time.</span></div>
</div>

<span style="font-family: inherit;"><span style="font-family: inherit;">I was planning on getting away once they were settled in.</span><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">It is not like I was in his lap.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">The children were wanting it and I needed the time for them to transition.......the call came in over 2 hours earlier than normal and I answered so I could make a special effort to pay attention to what you needed.</span></span></div>
</blockquote>
</a><div aria-label="#2... When I say you didn't understand... Or at least, you're not responding to what I'm really saying... 

This is NOT about Sunday night. You are focused on an event. That event was a catalyst combined with other things that leads to the real thing.

The real thing is: (1) My Hurting and (2) my coming to the realization that I need to change how I see you.

This is actually an example, now that I realize it, of a lack of skill talking at the emotional level. 

You are addressing the fact level and I am addressing an emotional wound.

All communication has two levels. Over the surface is facts and data. &quot;What's being said&quot;. Under the surface is emotional days &quot;what's really being said&quot;.

Sunday was my wake up call... But it's not about you giving our night away, not really.

What it's really about is me needing to accept you as &quot;just friends&quot; when I hadn't yet. And the pain that has caused me in the process. 

This isn't about an event, it's about me communicating my heart to you and wanting you to see it.

Maybe this could sound like &quot;I hear you saying that you are in pain, and it involves me... I hear your hurt. I am sorry you are hurting and that I may have contributed to your pain&quot;." class="_aok" style="font-family: inherit; outline: none;" tabindex="0">
<span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">#2... When I say you didn't understand... Or at least, you're not responding to what I'm really saying... 

This is NOT about Sunday night. You are focused on an event. That event was a catalyst combined with other things that leads to the real thing.

The real thing is: (1) My Hurting and (2) my coming to the realization that I need to change how I see you.

This is actually an example, now that I realize it, of a lack of skill talking at the emotional level. 

You are addressing the fact level and I am addressing an emotional wound.

All communication has two levels. Over the surface is facts and data. "What's being said". Under the surface is emotional days "what's really being said".

Sunday was my wake up call... But it's not about you giving our night away, not really.

What it's really about is me needing to accept you as "just friends" when I hadn't yet. And the pain that has caused me in the process. 

This isn't about an event, it's about me communicating my heart to you and wanting you to see it.

Maybe this could sound like "I hear you saying that you are in pain, and it involves me... I hear your hurt. I am sorry you are hurting and that I may have contributed to your pain".</span></div>
</div>
<span class="_2her _3zzf" style="background: none; border-radius: 50%; border: 1px solid currentcolor; bottom: 1px; box-sizing: border-box; color: #0084ff; display: inline-block; font-family: inherit; height: 10px; position: absolute; right: -15px; vertical-align: middle; width: 10px;" title="Sent"><i aria-label="Sent" aria-roledescription="Status icon" class="_57e_" role="img" style="border-color: currentcolor; border-image: initial; border-style: solid; border-width: 0px 0px 1px 1px; display: block; height: 1px; margin-left: 3px; margin-top: 4px; transform-origin: 0% 100%; transform: rotate(-45deg); width: 4px;"></i></span></div>

<span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">It's one of the other things I realized this week... <span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">If I am distant or awkward or respond weird... It's because I don't know what this new "us" means yet. I've already healed faster than I thought I would, God gave me that gift... But I'm still healing... If you were to visit right now and kiss me at the airport and then go back home to continue being single... I would die inside. I would stop all communication. I am not strong enough to handle that kind of mixed signals. So... You are welcome to visit someday... I want to see you... But... <span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">Also... I've already healed faster than I thought I would, God gave me that gift... But I'm still healing... <span style="font-family: inherit;"><span style="font-family: inherit;">Spirit stuff.</span><br /><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">Scott is present and working along side me and quite honestly sooooooo broken and unloved that he is getting more ministry from me and my mom that it does take up "spare time"</span><br /><span style="font-family: inherit;">The children have a big part in last weekend.</span></span></div>

</a><div aria-label="Now this one could actually be a lie... But when I read this I think: if I was really her &quot;best friend&quot; it wouldn't be spare time it would be before anything to do with him... Yep... Even as I say it that's at least partially a lie. But it also shows where I sit on your priorities. I'm spare time. It is what it is. Not knocking you for it, just acknowledging it for what it is. It wasn't spare time when we started. It was a quick text on the way to do something. I was on your mind and you had to take thirty seconds to share. I just don't occupy that place anymore. And a &quot;just friends&quot; probably shouldn't. So that's okay." class="_aok" style="font-family: inherit; outline: none;" tabindex="0">
<span class="_3oh- _58nk" style="font-family: inherit; user-select: text;">Now this one could actually be a lie... But it also shows where I sit on your priorities. I'm spare time. It is what it is. Not knocking you for it, just acknowledging it for what it is. It wasn't spare time when we started. It was a quick text on the way to do something. I was on your mind and you had to take thirty seconds to share. I just don't occupy that place anymore. And a "just friends" probably shouldn't. So that's okay.</span></div>
</div>
